title: neurocallis scandens
intro: Neurocallis scandens, commonly known as the climbing starflower, is a species of flowering plant in the family Campanulaceae. This plant is believed to have potential health benefits, including antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which may help to protect against oxidative stress and inflammation. The therapeutic actions of Neurocallis scandens include its possible use as an antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial agent, which may contribute to its potential in treating various health conditions. The bioactive constituents of Neurocallis scandens have been reported to include phenolic compounds, flavonoids, and alkaloids, which may be responsible for its medicinal properties.
side_effects: Neurocallis scandens can have side effects if used improperly, such as causing nausea and stomach discomfort in some individuals, especially when ingested in large quantities. Prolonged exposure to the plant's sap has been known to irritate the skin, leading to redness, itching, and in severe cases, allergic reactions. Inhaling the plant's volatile oils can also trigger respiratory issues, such as coughing and shortness of breath. Improper handling of Neurocallis scandens can also lead to headaches and dizziness due to the plant's toxic compounds.
